seed_problem: Partition a weighted graph into two balanced halves maximizing cut weight; checker validates balance and scores cut/baseline.
mutation: composite: relax the input to a large weighted general (multi)graph with a planted-but-scrambled heavy bipartition, and constrain the output to a size-balanced 2-partition (bowls differ by at most tol) -- so optimal balanced max-cut is NP-hard and the parity baseline is far from optimal, making greedy / swap-local-search / KL strategies diverge.
title: Hump Yard Split: Balanced Wagon Bowl Assignment
strategies: parity baseline: label wagon i by (i mod 2); always balanced but ignores separation gains
one-pass greedy: process wagons in order, place each in the bowl that maximizes marginal cut against already-placed neighbors, capping bowl sizes to keep balance
swap-based local search / Kernighan-Lin: from a greedy start, repeatedly swap the highest-gain cross-bowl wagon pair to climb the cut while preserving balance
randomized multi-restart local search: perturb assignments across seeds and keep the highest-gain balanced partition found
